## Structure Prediction Summary for TP53

**Overall Confidence:** medium (78.0%)

**Mutation:** R248W

TP53 (tumor protein p53) is the most frequently mutated gene in human cancers, with R248W being one of the six hotspot mutations in the DNA-binding domain. This gain-of-function mutation disrupts DNA binding and confers oncogenic properties. R248W is found in approximately 7.2% of TP53-mutant cancers.

Therapeutic options include APR-246 (Eprenetapopt), PC14586 (Rezatapopt), PRIMA-1.
